name: peer-review-final-gate
description: Final quality gate: enforce an independent peer-review-critic review of committed feature-branch work before a coding session is declared done. The last line of defence after code-review-gatekeeper.

enforcement.mechanism: Stop hook returns {"decision":"block","reason":...}; the model must launch peer-review-critic before the turn can end.
enforcement.fires: at most once per session (satisfied as soon as peer-review-critic has been invoked)

block_conditions.description: ALL must hold for the gate to block; any failure or uncertainty allows the stop (fail-open).
all_of: the Stop is not itself a stop-hook continuation (stop_hook_active is not true)
cwd is inside a git work tree
HEAD is a real branch — not detached and not the integration base branch
there are commits ahead of the integration base (origin/main, else origin/master, else main, else master)
the working tree is clean (a committed end-of-work milestone, not a mid-edit pause)
peer-review-critic has NOT been invoked this session (transcript marker "subagent_type":"peer-review-critic" absent)

remediation.action: Launch the peer-review-critic agent (Agent tool, subagent_type peer-review-critic) to review the branch diff against the base branch.
remediation.exit_criteria: Every BLOCKER and MAJOR finding resolved (or explicit user sign-off) before declaring the work done.

rationale: The Stop hook fires at the end of every turn, so the trigger is deliberately narrow (committed work on a feature branch, clean tree, not yet reviewed) to behave like a pre-merge review gate that runs once — not a per-turn nag. Pairs with tdd-workflow as the system's second and only other hard gate.
